name: wallet-selection-hl-copyability
description: Hyperliquid wallet-selection + rating config under the COPYABILITY-AT-SCALE lens: 'best' = the trader we can realistically and PROFITABLY MIRROR at our clip, not the trader with the gaudiest track record. It is a manual-review surfacing tool, NOT an autopilot copier (PERP_FOLLOW_STUDY_V2 proved exact-mirror copy of HL leaders pays -0.15%/trade and is dominated by our own regime detector at +1.93%/trade; persistence of the copyable segment is NEGATIVE, IC -0.50). So this config does not chase edge that doesn't carry forward; it screens for the operational PRECONDITIONS of profitable copy and surfaces the few names a human should review. Adapts the tail-discipline schema (wallet-selection-v0.1.0) to HL fill-derived metrics, re-weighting toward the five things that decide whether a copy is feasible: liquid-majors asset mix, mirrorable hold time, position size vs market depth, NET-of-copy-cost return (after taker fee + slippage + funding, i.e. after the documented -0.15%/trade copy tax), and a low add-depth / reserve requirement (a 54-add martingale is uncopyable without a huge idle reserve). BONUS for running an HL VAULT (zero-engineering protocol-native copy with enforced >=5% leader stake + 10% high-water profit-share; vault risk-adjusted persistence IC +0.223 was the ONLY non-inverting HL copy signal).
changelog: 0.1.0 — initial COPYABILITY-AT-SCALE candidate for HL. Add-depth thresholds recalibrated to the HL scale-in regime (HL traders scale positions across many fills; a Solana 20-add hardReject would reject the whole venue). Tail risk is bounded primarily by collateralMultiple, reserveMultiple, worstLossVsMedianWin, and liquidations rather than raw fill-add count. Net-of-copy-cost return (per the V2 copy-tax model: 4.5 bps taker + 3 bps/side slippage, both sides, on peak notional) is the load-bearing performance metric. Latency is intentionally NOT rewarded (V2: red herring, copy is negative at 0/5/15/30s).

0.1.1 — lowered eligibility.minClosedRoundTrips 8 -> 5 to surface near-miss operators with a short-but-clean record (e.g. 0x418aa6: 96% majors, 196h holds, +10%/trade net, reserveX 3.4, excluded under the old floor for having only 7 closed round-trips). The tail screen (reserveMultiple/worstLossVsMedianWin/liquidations + disqualifiers) still does the heavy lifting; the lower count floor only widens who gets SEEN, not who passes. Also lifted the grade-band/flag-threshold knobs that were previously hardcoded in rate_hl_copyability.py (letter bands, THIN_ALT/SUB_MINUTE/BLOW_UP flag cutoffs) into this JSON (flagThresholds + overall.gradeBands now genuinely read by the scorer) so the config is the single source of truth, and added a tradingActivity section so the EDT watch-window analytics built by consolidate-rated-wallets.mjs is config-driven too.

tradingActivity._doc: EDT trading-hours analytics config consumed by consolidate-rated-wallets.mjs when building each wallet's tradingActivity profile from cached fills. watchWindowEdt is the user's America/New_York 'can I actually WATCH this trader' window (DST-correct via Intl); daytimeActivePct = fill share inside it. overnightThreshold: daytimeActivePct below this -> TRADES_OVERNIGHT_EDT badge. peakHoursCount: how many top EDT hours to report. This is the copy-trading lens config, so the watch-window lives here and the consolidator reads watchWindowEdt back out for the UI.

copyCostModel._doc: The copier's friction we subtract from the leader's realized PnL to get NET-of-cost return. From PERP_FOLLOW_STUDY_V2: the copy tax is spread+fee+funding on entries with near-zero directional EV, ~-0.15%/trade even at zero latency with exact fill prices. Funding is already embedded in HL closedPnl and a same-direction copier pays the same sign, so we subtract only the copier's own entry+exit friction on mirrored peak notional.

eligibility._doc: Hard cutoffs to be scored. Below these = excluded (insufficient data to judge copyability), not graded F. HL fills retention caps history; require enough closed round-trips and span to assess hold time and consistency.

copyability._doc: PRIMARY criteria under this philosophy. These decide whether a copy is OPERATIONALLY FEASIBLE at our clip, independent of how good the headline record looks.

assetMix._doc: Share of traded notional in liquid majors (ETH/BTC). We can only mirror what we can fill without moving the book; thin-alt edge does not transfer to a follower's clip. majorsShare is ETH+BTC only; liquidShare adds SOL/HYPE (deep HL books).

holdTime._doc: Median hold per round-trip. Sub-minute scalps are UNCOPYABLE — by the time a follower fills, the leader is out, and the copy tax dwarfs any per-trade edge (V2: short holds are where even a correct directional call earns nothing after costs). We want holds long enough that a manual or low-frequency mirror can realistically participate. Hours, not seconds.

positionSizeVsDepth._doc: Median peak position notional. Two-sided: too SMALL (< $5k) means the leader is a dust account whose 'edge' is noise and whose sizing tells us nothing; too LARGE relative to HL book depth is not a copyability problem for US (we copy at our own clip, smaller), so the upper bound is informational. The grade rewards a sizing band that is real but still implies the leader is a serious operator. We do NOT penalize large size — we copy proportionally smaller.

netOfCostReturn._doc: CORE metric. Mean NET return per round-trip AFTER the copier's friction (copyCostModel) on peak notional. This is the closest single number to 'would mirroring this person have paid'. The bar is set ABOVE zero deliberately: a leader who only breaks even net-of-cost is not worth the operational risk, because the copy tax compounds and the V2 baseline (regime-alone) already returns +1.9%/trade for free. Also require a majority of trips net-positive so the edge isn't one lucky print.

addDepthAndReserve._doc: How much idle capital a copier must FRONT to survive the leader's escalating stack. CALIBRATION LESSON (v0.1.0): on HL, traders open with a tiny probe then scale to size, so BOTH raw fill-add count AND peak/first-fill 'collateral multiple' run to the hundreds even for clean operators (a $650 probe -> $360k position is normal scale-in, not a martingale). A Solana-style 'maxAddDepth>20' or 'collMult>15' reject would reject the entire venue. The copyability-honest escalation signal is reserveMultiple = max-trip peak notional / MEDIAN-trip peak notional (position-to-position, not probe-to-position): how far the worst stack blows past a typical position = the idle reserve a copier must hold. maxAddDepth and maxCollateralMultipleInfo are REPORTED for context but are NOT grade inputs.

tailSafety._doc: The blow-up signature (V2: 90% of the HL active universe blows up, median worst-loss ~34x median win). A copier inherits the leader's tail at full leverage. Bound the worst realized loss relative to the typical win, and treat realized liquidations as a tail that already fired.

openPositionGuard._doc: Live open round-trips are where a martingale hides its bomb (the Wallet2 / -$33k live-stack lesson). FLAG-ONLY in v0.1.0, NOT a hard disqualifier: HL's fills endpoint retains only ~3.5d of recent 1m and a bounded fill window, so 'open' round-trips are detected imperfectly and the open-vs-median ratio is noisy when the live position is on a coin the wallet rarely trades (low median). We surface LIVE_DEEP_STACK as a manual-review flag and dock one grade, but let reserveMultiple + worstLossVsMedianWin + liquidations carry the hard tail screen. Tighten to a hard reject once a reliable live-position snapshot (clearinghouseState) is ingested.

vaultBonus._doc: BONUS (not a requirement). Running an HL VAULT is the zero-engineering protocol-native copy path: deposit USDC, get proportional exposure, no mirror engine, no DEX aggregator. It also enforces skin-in-the-game (leader >=5% stake) + aligned fee (10% high-water profit-share) + withdrawal lockup, which self-selects operators over gamblers. The vault risk-adjusted persistence IC (+0.223) was the ONLY HL copy signal that never inverted across regime breaks. NOTE: in the cached universe NONE of the 43 persistent leaderboard accounts lead a vault (vault-overlap.json), so this bonus currently fires on no one in the shortlist — it is forward-looking for when vault-led operators are ingested.

ratingRubric._doc: Per-category letter grades A-F (A=4..F=0). Overall = weighted average of category 0-10 scores, +vaultBonus, then any autoDisqualifier floors overall to F. Weights re-centered on COPYABILITY: asset mix, hold time, and net-of-cost return carry the most because they decide feasibility; tail safety guards the inherited risk; size and add/reserve are the operational frictions.

consistencyModifier._doc: Secondary guard against one-burst records (the Wallet2 problem). Applied as a small multiplier to netOfCostReturn, not its own category. Reward net profit spread across sub-periods, penalize concentration.

copyabilityAtScale._doc: The headline practical badge: the idle reserve (x median peak notional) a copier must hold to survive the leader's deepest stack at the target scale. Lower = more copyable. Directly = reserveMultiple from the metrics.

autoDisqualifiers._doc: Any TRUE => overall grade F + 'DISQUALIFIED' flag, regardless of category averages. Each encodes a copyability kill-switch. 'rules' is the human-readable list; 'thresholds' is the machine-readable form the scorer actually evaluates (so the JSON, not the Python, is the source of truth).

flagThresholds._doc: Cutoffs for the SOFT badge flags (not disqualifiers). Previously hardcoded in rate_hl_copyability.py; lifted here so every knob is config-tweakable. THIN_ALT_TRADER: majorsShare below; SUB_MINUTE_SCALPER: subMinuteFrac at/above; NET_NEGATIVE_AFTER_COPY_COST: meanNetRetPerTrade at/below; BLOW_UP_RISK fires if worstLossVsMedianWin above OR liquidations at/above; DEEP_MARTINGALE: reserveMultiple above.

overall._doc: Emit BOTH a letter and a 0-10 score per category and overall. A disqualified wallet shows F/0 with the breached rule(s) listed. The vaultBonus adds up to +0.5 letter-points (capped at A) BEFORE the disqualifier check.

uiHints._doc: For the HL trader-browser UI: which fields to surface so a human can scan and shortlist for MANUAL follow/no-follow. This is a decision-support tool, not an autopilot.
uiHints.disclaimer: Surfaced wallets are MANUAL-REVIEW candidates. HL copy persistence is negative (IC -0.50); a high grade means 'copy was operationally feasible historically', NOT 'copying this will be profitable'.
